We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agriculture Officer to determine the existence and validity of the recorded asset including the identification of the person accountable thereof. Further, require necessary adjustment in the books to derecognize such asset, if warranted, in accordance with IPSAS 1 and 27.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal     Accountant to:
Exert effort to retrieve the details and supporting documents of the outstanding payables, and review/analyze the same to determine valid claims; and
prepare and send confirmation letter/s for payables with details and identified creditors/claimants, and require the immediate submission of the necessary supporting documents to facilitate payment of the accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to compute and observe the correct recognition of depreciation expense of the PPE accounts for the current year and prior period adjustment to reflect the correct balances in the year-end financial statements.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant will provide depreciation expenses/accumulated depreciation for all recorded PPE accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Ensure that the development projects funded under the 20% Development Fund are well-planned, procurement- and- implementation ready and properly coordinated with the concerned departments;
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office, and regularly monitor the implementation of the targeted development projects funded out of 20% DF; and
Ensure that the 20% Development Fund is fully utilized in order that the target beneficiaries could timely avail of the benefits and usage of the projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMO will observe the guidelines of DILG MC No. 2012-73 and NDRRMC, DBM, DILG and CSC JMC No. 2014 - 01 to make sure that all disbursements charged to the LDRRMF will be made in accordance with the required guidelines and its intended purpose.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMO observe the guidelines of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-01 and to make sure that all PPAs funded out of LDRRMF will be implemented in accordance with the required guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to implement the unimplemented projects, as embodied in the MOA with the SAs, to avoid further delay in the implementation of the projects.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant revisit the MOA or in its absence coordinate with the SAs to better facilitate the return of excess fund of those projects that were already completed, which is considered long overdue to derecognize the said liability and to make the funds available for other priority projects of the SAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:
Prepare RAAF and record all the Accountable Forms in her custody including those which are kept in the vault;
Include/record the checks in the RAAF of the Accountable Officers and eventually in the CRAAF; and
Prepare the CRAAF in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU, Volume II by recording and consolidating the data in the RAAF of each AO.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
GSO-Designate - to exert effort to retrieve documents/data needed in the processing of insurance coverage of insurable properties with the GIF of the GSIS and to secure adequate insurance coverage for all the uninsured properties. Additionally, submit yearly the PIF to the GIF of the GSIS and to the Office of the Supervising Auditor/Audit Team Leader every 30th day of April; and
Budget Officer - to include in the annual budget a sufficient amount for the payment of property insurance premium based on the billing statements from the GSIS. In the event of budgetary constraint, the payment of premium on calamity insurance may be charged against the LDRRMF pursuant to Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to submit on time all the records of transactions with supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the timely preparation of the financial statements and other required reports.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the complete Financial Statements, its supporting schedules, and other financial reports of the Municipality within the prescribed period as required by P.D. No. 1445 to timely verify and examine the financial condition and operating performance of the Municipality as basis for rendering the appropriate audit opinion.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the concerned Department Heads/employees to formulate strategies/action plan to implement audit recommendations contained in CY 2022 AAR.
Assign a focal person to consolidate the action plans and prepare the AAPSI; and
Strictly observe submission of the AAPSI to the Audit Team within sixty (60) days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report, submit to the Audit Team the duly accomplished AAPSI with supporting documents, and likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

1.Effect necessary adjusting entries in the books to reclassify all completed infrastructure projects still recorded under the CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts:
2. Compute the corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation for all completed projects as of December 31, 2022 and adjust the affected accounts as of year-end.

3. Closely coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to obtain the status of on-going projects to properly classify the asset accounts as of year-end.

Require the Municipal Engineer to constantly update the Municipal Accountant on the status of accomplishments of on-going infrastructure projects for accurate presentation of related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Designate a responsible official to facilitate the processing of the Certificate of Title of the 96 parcels of land owned by the Municipality in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality over these properties:

Create an Appraisal Committee composed of the Municipal Assessor, Engineer, Accountant and Acting Treasurer to facilitate the appraisal of the value of the parcels of land owned by the municipality;

c.  Trace/find the original title of lands owned by the Municipality as shown in the tax declaration and file the same.

d. Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to furnish a copy of TCTs to the Office of the Municipal Assessor in order to reconcile their files.

e. Direct the OIC- Assessor to update their records.

f. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of account the unrecorded land properties of the Municipality;

g. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards for the land account; and

h. Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain Property Cards for the parcels of land and other real properties owned by the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile the above balances and furnish the Audit Team with the necessary records and documents to validate the adjustments; and

Properly monitor loans granted to the Municipality  so that it will be fairly presented and obligated.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the fund transfers received from source agencies are supported with duly signed Memorandum of Agreement and related documents, before allowing any disbursements and make certain that funds are utilized only for its intended purpose.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the:

a. The Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs Volume I; and

GSO to ensure that issuance of supplies and materials are supported with Requisition and Issue Slip, and prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

a. Expedite institution of a fully automated payroll system to develop a sound internal control as prescribed under Section 124 of PD No. 1445 and Item 2 of COA Circular No. 76-26A; and

b. Continue to monitor, implement and install a sound system of internal control particularly those in connection with disbursement of all forms to safeguard the funds of the Municipality.

c. Ensure that the DO is properly bonded according to her cash accountability.

We recommended and the management agreed to instruct the MENRO – Designate and personnel concerned to:

Ensure that the “No segregation, No Collection” policy shall be implemented; and

Encourage all Barangays to organize their own Bantay Basura Enforcement Team to have a coordinated SWM in the Municipality of Dumangas.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the GFPS to properly monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs in order to readily identify:

Those that were implemented to be reported in the accomplishment report;

Priority PPAs which do not duplicate with the national budget supported programs; and

Those activities that were not implemented in order that appropriate action is taken thereon.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct:

The concerned Rural Health Unit personnel to prepare the Inventory Report on the remaining undistributed vaccines as of year-end with information as to valuation and furnish the copy to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

The Municipal Accountant to record the transaction on the agency books based on the complete Inventory Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the responsible persons/office/department the concerned Department Heads/employees to formulate strategies/action plan to implement audit recommendations contained in CY 2021 AAR.

Require the focal person to consolidate the action plans and prepare the AAPSI; and

Strictly observe submission of the AAPSI to the Audit Team within sixty (60) days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report in accordance with the pertinent section of the General Appropriations Act (GAA).

      the Inventory Report and the books of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE direct  the  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer  to
   deposit intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not later  than
   the next of banking day to avoid possible loss or misuse of the fund  and
   as required under pertinent provisions.

   employees to  liquidate  and/or  refund  immediately  their  unused  cash
   advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 339 of RA 7160 in relation
   with Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and  96-004,
   to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a  fair
   presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.

   pertinent provisions that no  cash  advances  shall  be  allowed  to  any
   official or employee unless the previous cash advance  granted  is  first
   settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.


   We further recommended for the imposition of  appropriate  sanctions  for
   the unjustified failure of  the  concerned  officials  and  employees  to
   liquidate their cash advances.

   a. To exhaust all means to  retrieve  the  necessary  data/documents  for
      immediate recording  in  the  books  of  account,  of  all  identified
      reconciling items;

      require  adjustment  and  correction  in  the  GL  promptly  for  fair
      presentation in the FS;


   c. To communicate and reconcile with the bank  the  Identified  erroneous

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct: The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Auditor provided under the Handbook on PSMS and COA Circular No. 92-386; That reconciliation be made between the RPCPPE and accounting records to ascertain the existence and correctness of the reported balances totaling P237,188,069.01 in the financial statements; and The Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries for the recognition of 25% of Public Infrastructure for CY 2016.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts in all funds maintained by the Municipality, by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and to effect immediate, proper and verifiable adjustments in the books of accounts. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure the release of the RPT shares directly to the Barangay Treasurers on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter in strict compliance with Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit immediately all the taxes due to the BIR, otherwise, penalties on late remittances will be a personal liability of the persons responsible. Henceforth, all taxes withheld shall be remitted within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made.

We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor. We also recommended that the LCE shall henceforth, submit the properly accomplished AAPSI, within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR, to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the LCE with the support of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the submission of quarterly reports on GPPAs and information and publicity on such PPAs to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the public.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare and submit the required reports within the reglementary period. We also recommended that the Municipality adhere to DILG MC No. 2012-73 in the utilization of its Local DRRM Fund to maximize the purpose of the Fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the Taytay sa Kauswagan, Inc. to submit the required reports and supporting documents for evaluation as stated in the MOA and COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the responsible LGU personnel to coordinate with the NGAs concerned regarding the required terms and provisions to be incorporated in the MOA pertaining to the fund transfer, specifically the projects to be implemented and schedules of implementation, for proper monitoring of the progress of the projects and evaluating the propriety of its implementation and the utilization of funds for the purposes intended.

We recommended that the LCE direct the persons concerned to observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the persons responsible to properly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-010 relative to the information needed to be shown in the purchase orders and ensure that the quality and specifications of the items delivered correspond to the value paid by the agency and essentially meet the needs of the end users.

We recommended that the LCE direct the preparation/submission of the accomplishment report for evaluation of the implementation of the GAD programs and activities, and observe strictly the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the LCE direct the personnel responsible for the procurement of the SMS Alert System, to submit the complete supporting documents required under the pertinent provision of RA No. 9184, and other relevant proofs/documentary evidences to establish the propriety/validity of the mode of procurement adopted by the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the responsible  LGU
personnel to coordinate with the NGAs concerned  regarding  the  required
terms and provisions to be incorporated in the MOA pertaining to the fund
transfer, specifically the projects to be implemented  and  schedules  of
implementation, for proper monitoring of the progress of the projects and
evaluating the propriety of its implementation  and  the  utilization  of
funds for the purposes intended.

Recommendations:

We recommended that the LCE  direct  the  persons  concerned  to  observe
strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section  A.2  of
COA Circular No. 96-010  by  submitting  a  copy  of  all  contracts  and
purchase orders within five (5) working  days  from  its  execution  and
notify the Auditor of the time  and  date  of  the  scheduled  deliveries
within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the LCE direct the persons  responsible  to  properly
observe the provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  96-010  relative  to  the
information needed to be shown in the purchase orders and ensure that the
quality and specifications of the items delivered correspond to the value
paid by the agency and essentially meet the needs of the end users.

Recommendation:

We recommended that the LCE  direct  the  preparation/submission  of  the
accomplishment report for evaluation of the  implementation  of  the  GAD
programs and activities, and observe strictly the provisions of the  PCW,
DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Auditor provided under the Handbook on PSMS and COA Circular No. 92-386, to eliminate doubt on the existence, condition and correctness of the total value of PPE owned by the Municipality. Likewise, we recommended that reconciliation be made between the RPCPPE and accounting records to ascertain the existence and correctness of the reported balances totaling P213,829,350.20 in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that the LCE refrain from granting cash advances to officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances and strictly adhere to the guidelines in granting the same. We further recommended for the imposition of appropriate sanctions for the unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record cash donations received for the victims of Typhoon Yolanda and the unexpended /unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2015-002. Necessary adjusting entries should be effected to correct the original accounting entries made.
We recommended that the SEF be solely and exclusively used for programs and activities embodied in RA Nos. 5447 and 7160 to maximize the benefits for the students in the Municipality of Dumangas.

We recommended that all development projects be utilized during the year as embodied in the Annual Development Plan. We likewise recommended that disbursements for the 20% Development Fund shall be in accordance with the guidelines set in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the LCE direct the persons concerned to observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days from its execution and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of such deliveries.

We recommended that the LCE direct the creation of its Municipal GFPS who shall spearhead the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR and observe strictly the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments to properly classify the accounts based on the account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of her working papers on the conversion of accounts to ascertain its correctness.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the provisions of Sections 122 of PD 1445 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace back the cause of differences and effect the necessary adjustments to reconcile the same. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis to correct the difference as early as possible and avoid the buildup of discrepancies over time.
